statement: Bitcoin will be above 100K on May 1
Pokemon SV8 (Surging Sparks successor) set release will increase Cambridge TCG monthly revenue by >15% in its release month vs the prior month
source: Historical pattern: every major Pokemon set release (SV6 Twilight Masquerade, SV7 Stellar Crown, SV8) has driven 15-30% revenue spikes for TCG retailers. Cambridge TCG current baseline GBP3200/mo. SV8 expected Q3 2026. High confidence based on repeatable seasonal pattern.

statement: UK trading card market total value will grow >10% YoY in 2026 vs 2025
source: UK TCG market has grown 12-18% YoY since 2020 driven by Pokemon, One Piece, and Lorcana. Some deceleration expected as post-COVID boom normalizes, but new IP entries (Dragon Ball Fusion World, Star Wars Unlimited) sustain growth. 65% confidence accounts for possible macro headwinds (UK consumer spending, inflation).

statement: eBay will announce an increase in seller fees (final value fee or insertion fee) for Trading Cards category before end of 2026
source: eBay has raised seller fees in 5 of the last 7 years. Trading Cards category currently at 13.25% FVF. eBay under pressure to grow revenue post-TCGPlayer acquisition. 60% confidence — fee increases are likely but TCG category specifically could be shielded to compete with TCGPlayer integration. Revenue decision: if fees rise, margin compression makes direct-sales channels more valuable.

statement: Ethereum spot price will be above USD 3,000 on July 1, 2026
source: ETH has been volatile in 2026. Current price range ~2200-2800. Ethereum Pectra upgrade and potential ETF inflows are bullish catalysts, but macro uncertainty (Fed policy, geopolitical tensions) creates headwinds. 55% confidence reflects genuine uncertainty. Revenue decision: ETH price affects Zerone launch timing — bull market = more favorable for token launches, bear market = build quietly.

statement: At least 3 new blockchains explicitly using Proof of Useful Work consensus (not PoW or PoS) will launch public testnets in 2026
source: Multiple projects building PoUW chains: Gensyn (ML training), Ritual (AI inference), Bittensor subnets expanding. Strong VC funding in AI+crypto intersection. 72% confidence — the trend is clear but 'public testnet' is a specific bar some may not clear in 2026. Revenue decision: validates Zerone's Proof of Truth approach as timely. More competitors = larger market category = easier narrative for ZRN. Track which specific use-cases they target vs Zerone's knowledge verification niche.

statement: Anthropic will release a model with >1M token context window available as default (not limited preview) via API by end of Q3 2026
source: Claude currently offers 200K standard, 1M in limited/extended contexts. GPT-4.1 at 1M, Gemini at 2M. Competitive pressure is strong. Anthropic's trajectory shows steady context expansion. 62% confidence — technically achievable but Anthropic may prioritize other capabilities (agents, tools) over raw context. Revenue decision: larger context windows directly improve AI Services offering — can process entire codebases, full legal documents, complete market datasets in single calls. Affects pricing and capability of Kingdom AI Services.

statement: UK will pass legislation or binding regulation requiring disclosure/labelling of AI-generated content in commercial use before end of 2026
source: UK AI Safety Institute established but government has favored voluntary frameworks over hard regulation. EU AI Act sets precedent but UK explicitly chose divergent path. Labour government more regulation-friendly but legislative pipeline is slow. 35% confidence — political will exists but parliamentary time is scarce and lobby pressure from UK AI sector (DeepMind, etc) favors lighter touch. Revenue decision: mandatory AI disclosure would affect Seigei, AI Services pricing, and client acquisition. If passed, early compliance = competitive advantage.

statement: Kingdom OS will have >3 active revenue-generating engines (defined as >GBP 100/mo revenue each) by September 1, 2026
source: Currently only 1 engine generating revenue (Cambridge TCG at GBP3200/mo). Candidates for activation: Oracle (needs track record before capital deployment), AI Services (0 clients, targeting 3), Seigei (private beta, pre-revenue), Shopify Apps (paused). Getting from 1 to 4 revenue engines in 5 months is ambitious. 40% confidence — each engine has a plausible path to GBP100/mo but multiple simultaneous activations face bandwidth constraints with the Triarchy. Revenue decision: if this resolves FALSE, it signals over-extension and need to focus deeper on fewer engines.

statement: Cambridge TCG will achieve GBP 3,840/mo revenue (the +20% target from GBP 3,200 baseline) for at least one month by June 30, 2026
source: Current baseline GBP3200/mo. Target is +20% = GBP3840. Automation pipeline (procurement optimization, listing automation, market intelligence) is in progress. Historical Q2 is strong for TCG (new set releases, summer demand). 70% confidence — the target is achievable through operational improvements alone without needing market tailwinds, but execution risk remains (automation still being built). Revenue decision: this is THE core metric for Phase 1. If FALSE by June, reassess automation strategy and timeline.

statement: Average asking price for agricultural farmland in Suffolk will exceed GBP 10,000 per acre by end of 2026
source: Current Suffolk farmland averages GBP 8,500-9,500/acre (Savills/Knight Frank 2025 data). UK farmland has appreciated 3-5% annually. Drivers: inflation hedge demand, renewable energy competition for land, inheritance tax changes driving sales. Counter: high interest rates suppress leveraged buyers. 58% confidence — the trend is upward but GBP10K is a specific threshold that may not be crossed in a single year. Revenue decision: directly affects Kingdom farmland acquisition timing and budget. If prices accelerate, earlier purchase is better. If they stall, patience is rewarded.
